tests.intent: Two runs use distinct unpredictable Compose project names, newly created credential files and volumes, begin with no project resources, and always destroy volumes and secrets on every exit path.
Database, ORDS and evaluator image/build/start/health/shutdown operations have reviewed hard deadlines; timeout, unhealthy, restart, OOM, crash, or missing health evidence fails rather than skips.
Live inspection proves the database is capped at exactly two CPUs and two GiB, production images use reviewed digests, and only the evaluator can reach DB and ORDS on internal test networks.
The evaluator is non-root, read-only, capability-free, no-new-privileges, without Docker socket, host namespaces or writable source mounts, and has only bounded tmpfs scratch plus test credentials.
Every visible P0 through T10 SQL, API, simulation, mutation and Playwright family is executed exactly once per fresh run from inside the evaluator container with immutable reviewed commands.
Every command must exit zero and emit its expected nonempty PASS records; timeout, signal, malformed output, skip, not-run, todo, empty assertion count, duplicate, missing, or unexpected suite fails.

SQL acceptance originates inside the evaluator over an explicit test-only database transport and cannot shell through Docker, reuse host sqlplus output, or substitute static/source checks for live assertions.

API acceptance originates inside the evaluator over the same ORDS service and records actual status, encoding and payload hashes without proxy, fixture server, route interception, or prerecorded responses.

Pinned Chromium executes the reviewed browser projects inside the evaluator with zero retries, forbidOnly, snapshot updates disabled, no skipped tests, no request failures, and validated JSON reports.

All fourteen local core rows from the final acceptance matrix have direct named evidence and GREEN status; Cloud and Performance remain explicitly later-phase rows and no partial matrix is called complete.

A byte-level allowlist audit accepts only compiled production client and declared SQL artifacts, rejects source maps, TypeScript, evaluator/reference/fixture coupling, debug markers and undeclared executable content.

Live catalog audit finds zero invalid objects, zero disabled or unvalidated constraints, exact reviewed AutoREST exposure, least grants, no evaluator-shaped objects, and expected production object/count fingerprints.

Each run emits every reviewed independent correctness hash domain exactly once as lowercase SHA-256 over canonical observations, never log text, timestamps, container ids, compressed nondeterminism, or evaluator expectations.

The second independent fresh-volume bootstrap produces byte-identical canonical correctness, capability, schema and production-artifact ledgers while run identity and infrastructure evidence remain separately auditable.
Generated test credentials never occur in command lines, inspect output, logs, artifacts, reports or ledgers; repository secrets remain ignored and no secret-bearing file survives cleanup.

Focused orchestration and evidence mutations are killed by named assertions, evaluator self/foundation/adversarial controls pass, and frozen upstream plus local evaluator inputs match approved SHA-256 ancestry.
